# Answer format guide

## 6-mark answer structure
1. State the theorem or definition clearly (1 mark)
2. Identify the key assumption or hypothesis (1 mark)
3. Provide the derivation or proof outline (3 marks)
4. State the conclusion or applied example (1 mark)

## 12-mark answer structure
1. Statement of the result with hypothesis (2 marks)
2. Full derivation showing intermediate steps (5 marks)
3. Applied example demonstrating use (3 marks)
4. Edge case or boundary behaviour discussion (2 marks)

The 12 mark answer requires statement derivation applied example and edge case. Skipping any of these four loses marks proportionally even if the math is correct.

## Common deductions
- Skipping the hypothesis statement loses 2 marks regardless of answer length
- Mechanical derivation without intuition loses 1 mark on full questions
- Missing units or dimension check on applied example loses 1 mark
